title: Public shape factory functions completely untyped
description: rectangle(), ellipse(), and triangle() are public API functions with zero type annotations on parameters or return values. All parameters (fill_color, stroke_color, gradient_stops, etc.) default to untyped values. mypy --strict reports [no-untyped-def] for all three. Callers passing wrong types (e.g. a string for fill_color) get no type-checker feedback.
suggestion: Add full parameter and return annotations. Return type is dict[str, Any]. Parameters like fill_color should be Color | None, gradient_stops should be Sequence[tuple[float, Color]] | None, etc.

title: Public callout factory functions text() and square() completely untyped
description: text() and square() are high-level public API functions for creating callout annotations. Neither has any parameter or return type annotations. mypy --strict reports [no-untyped-def] for both. These are among the most commonly used annotation APIs.
suggestion: Add full parameter and return annotations. text should be str, font_name str, font_weight str, font_size float, font_color Color | None, etc. Return type is dict[str, Any].

title: Subpackage __init__.py files missing __all__, breaking strict re-exports
description: library/__init__.py and media_bin/__init__.py re-export symbols via 'from .module import X' but lack __all__. Under mypy --strict, re-exports without __all__ are not considered public, causing [attr-defined] errors in every downstream importer (project.py, extras.py, clips/group.py). This produces 8 cascading errors in mypy --strict output.
suggestion: Add __all__ = ['Library', 'LibraryAsset', 'Libraries', 'import_libzip', 'export_libzip'] to library/__init__.py and __all__ = ['Media', 'MediaBin', 'MediaType'] to media_bin/__init__.py.

title: 31 unused # type: ignore comments across the codebase
description: 31 '# type: ignore' comments suppress errors that no longer exist. These were likely needed during initial development but the underlying issues have been resolved. They now mask future real type errors. Affected files include: effects/visual.py (2), effects/cursor.py (2), timeline/clips/base.py (8), timeline/track.py (8), project.py (1), builders/slide_import.py (1), export/captions.py (1), media_bin/media_bin.py (1), cli.py (2), and others.
suggestion: Remove all 31 unused type: ignore comments. Run `python -m mypy src/camtasia --strict 2>&1 | grep unused-ignore` to get the full list. Consider adding --warn-unused-ignores to the default mypy config.

title: with_undo() decorator return type is bare Callable, erasing decorated function signatures
description: The with_undo() decorator at line 215 declares its return type as bare 'Callable' without type arguments. This means any function decorated with @with_undo loses its type signature from the perspective of callers. mypy --strict reports [type-arg] for the missing generic parameters.
suggestion: Use ParamSpec + Callable[P, T] for full signature preservation, or at minimum Callable[[Callable[..., T]], Callable[..., T]] to preserve the return type.

title: Track methods __contains__, filter_clips, and apply_to_all missing parameter annotations
description: Track.__contains__(item) at line 211 has no type for 'item'. Track.filter_clips(predicate) at line 1940 has no type for 'predicate'. Track.apply_to_all(fn) at line 2589 has no type for 'fn'. These are public API methods. mypy --strict reports [no-untyped-def] for all three.
suggestion: __contains__ should accept int | BaseClip | object. filter_clips and apply_to_all should type their callable parameter as Callable[[BaseClip], bool] and Callable[[BaseClip], None] respectively.

title: Timeline.apply_to_all_clips() missing parameter annotation for fn
description: Timeline.apply_to_all_clips(fn) at line 895 has no type annotation for the 'fn' parameter. This is a public API method that delegates to Track.apply_to_all(). mypy --strict reports [no-untyped-def].
suggestion: Type fn as Callable[[BaseClip], None] to match Track.apply_to_all.

title: TypedDict fields use bare dict without type arguments
description: EffectDict.parameters (line 404), TransitionDict.attributes (line 413), and HealthCheckResult.statistics (line 433) are typed as bare 'dict' without type arguments. Under mypy --strict this triggers [type-arg]. These are internal types but they propagate to public API return values.
suggestion: Use dict[str, Any] for parameters and attributes. For HealthCheckResult.statistics, consider a more specific TypedDict or dict[str, int | str].

title: Effect.metadata property returns bare dict
description: The metadata property at line 55 is annotated as returning 'dict' without type arguments. This is a public property on the base Effect class inherited by all effect subclasses.
suggestion: Change return type to dict[str, Any].

title: MediaBin uses Any for project, timeline, and library parameters where Protocol or TYPE_CHECKING imports would work
description: MediaBin.__init__ takes project: Any, unused_media takes timeline: Any, and add_to_library takes library: Any and returns Any. All three have known interfaces: project has track_changes(), timeline has all_clips(), library has add_asset(). Using Any defeats type checking at all call sites.
suggestion: Use TYPE_CHECKING imports with the concrete types (Project, Timeline, Library) or define Protocol classes for the required interfaces to avoid circular imports while preserving type safety.

title: _to_ticks parameter typed as Any when int | str | Fraction would suffice
description: _to_ticks(v: Any) converts tick values to int. The actual input domain is int | str | Fraction | None based on the implementation (int(Fraction(str(v)))). Using Any prevents catching callers that pass completely wrong types.
suggestion: Type v as int | str | Fraction | None.

title: ClipData.src typed as Any when int | None would be more precise
description: The src field in the _ClipData TypedDict is typed as Any. Based on usage across the codebase (source_id property returns int | None, comparisons with media IDs), the actual type is int | None. Using Any here propagates imprecision to every clip property that reads src.
suggestion: Change src: Any to src: int | None, or src: int | str | None if string source IDs are possible.

title: Pervasive bare dict/list/tuple in validation.py (26 occurrences)
description: validation.py uses bare dict, list, and tuple without type arguments in 26 locations. While this module is internal, it is the single largest contributor to the 177 mypy --strict errors. All are [type-arg] violations.
suggestion: Add type arguments throughout. Most are dict[str, Any] and list[str]. Consider a batch fix since the pattern is uniform.
